## Sample size: N = 3010
## log(100) = 4.605170
## Mean diff (should equal log(100)): 4.605170
## somecol: mean = 0.5053 (share with > 12 yrs schooling)
## educ16:  mean = 0.2714 (share with >= 16 yrs schooling)
## nearc4 (Z): mean = 0.6821 (first stage >  0 required)
## Region variables found: reg661, reg662, reg663, reg664, reg665, reg666, reg667, reg668, reg669 
## 
## ==============================================================================
## CARD (1995) REPLICATION — SUW TABLE 3
## ==============================================================================
## 
## Estimating propensity scores...
##   Done.
## 
## --- Treatment: somecol (educ > 12) ---
##   Computing column: col1: somecol, cents, Card
##   Computing column: col2: somecol, dollars, Card
##   Computing column: col3: somecol, cents, Kitagawa
##   Computing column: col4: somecol, dollars, Kitagawa
## 
## --- Treatment: educ16 (educ >= 16) ---
##   Computing column: col5: educ16, cents, Card
##   Computing column: col6: educ16, dollars, Card
##   Computing column: col7: educ16, cents, Kitagawa
##   Computing column: col8: educ16, dollars, Kitagawa
## 
## ==============================================================================
## Table 3. Causal effects of college education on log wages.
## Replication of Słoczyński, Uysal & Wooldridge (2025), Table 3.
## ==============================================================================
##                          (1)       (2)       (3)       (4)       (5)       (6)       (7)       (8)
## Treatment:           somecol   somecol   somecol   somecol    educ16    educ16    educ16    educ16
## Outcome:               cents   dollars     cents   dollars     cents   dollars     cents   dollars
## Spec:                   Card      Card  Kitagawa  Kitagawa      Card      Card  Kitagawa  Kitagawa
## ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ 
## 
## Panel A: 2SLS
## 2SLS                   0.661     0.661     0.575     0.575     1.392     1.392     0.991     0.991
##                        0.295     0.295     0.308     0.308     0.801     0.801     0.611     0.611  [SE]
## 
## Panel B: Normalized kappa estimators
## tau_cb_u               0.376     0.376     0.331     0.331     0.853     0.853     0.588     0.588
##                        0.223     0.223     0.236     0.236     0.549     0.549     0.433     0.433  [SE]
## tau_ml_u               0.331     0.331     0.356     0.356     0.619     0.619     0.628     0.628
##                        0.202     0.202     0.244     0.244     0.387     0.387     0.448     0.448  [SE]
## tau_ml_a10             0.346     0.346     0.293     0.293     0.586     0.586     0.836     0.836
##                        0.200     0.200     0.252     0.252     0.356     0.356     0.821     0.821  [SE]
## 
## Panel C: Unnormalized kappa estimators
## tau_ml_a              -0.319     0.170     2.248     0.842    -0.594     0.315     4.317     1.617
##                        1.182     0.370     0.971     0.362     2.184     0.696     2.485     0.891  [SE]
## tau_ml_t              -0.321     0.171     2.053     0.769    -0.601     0.319     3.651     1.367
##                        1.201     0.367     0.813     0.308     2.251     0.687     1.780     0.648  [SE]
## tau_ml_a0             -0.290     0.154     2.846     1.066    -0.501     0.266     7.241     2.712
##                        1.036     0.354     1.592     0.574     1.728     0.639     7.246     2.577  [SE]
## ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ 
## Notes:
##   2SLS: HC1-robust SEs (vcovHC type='HC1'), matches Stata ivreg2 vce(robust).
##   Kappa: M-estimation sandwich SEs from SUW (2025) online appendix formulas.
##   Propensity score: logit MLE (tau_ml_*) or CBPS (tau_cb_u).
##   Card spec: exper, expersq, black, south, smsa, smsa66, reg661-reg668.
##   Kitagawa spec: black, south, smsa, smsa66, south66.
##   Significance: *** p<0.01, ** p<0.05, * p<0.10 (two-sided z-test).
## 
##

## ==============================================================================
# ==============================================================================
# CARD (1995) — DML EXTENSION
# Builds on card_1995_replication.R — all toolkit functions must be in memory
# before running this script. Source card_1995_replication.R first.
# ==============================================================================
#
# WHY ONLY LOG WAGES IN DOLLARS FOR DML?
# ----------------------------------------
# The kappa replication runs both cents and dollars to demonstrate translation
# invariance (Panel C failure for unnormalized estimators). For DML this
# diagnostic is not needed: PLR-IV and Wald-AIPW are translation invariant by
# construction (sum(omega_i) == 0 exactly, as shown in Block D below). Running
# both outcome units would only confirm what is already known algebraically.
# Therefore, DML is run on lwage = log(wage/100) (dollars) only. The kappa
# estimates in the summary table come from card_1995_replication.R and cover
# both outcome units for comparison.
#
# NOTE on DML specifications vs. Card specifications:
#   The Card design has a richer covariate set than Angrist (1990). Rather than
#   running a "sparse" spec analogous to linear age, we run two DML specs:
#
#   BLOCK B — Card spec:    exper, expersq, black, south, smsa, smsa66, reg dummies
#             This is a medium-dimensional, mostly discrete/binary X. A random
#             forest can adapt to interactions among these binary variables.
#
#   BLOCK C — Kitagawa spec: black, south, smsa, smsa66, south66
#             This is a parsimonious 5-variable spec. In the low-dimensional
#             limit, DML and kappa should agree more closely. The comparison
#             between Block B and Block C illustrates how the richer Card spec
#             can produce different ML-based estimates.
#
#   BOTH BLOCKS are run for BOTH treatments (somecol, educ16), giving 4 DML
#   fits in total.
#
# STRUCTURE (run BLOCKS in order; B and C are independent of each other):
#   BLOCK A  — Setup, X matrices, helper functions
#   BLOCK B  — DML: Card specification (main spec for Love plots + diagnostics)
#              B1: somecol (D1),  B2: educ16 (D2)
#   BLOCK C  — DML: Kitagawa specification
#              C1: somecol (D1),  C2: educ16 (D2)
#   BLOCK D  — Translation invariance: DML vs kappa (all 4 DML fits)
#   BLOCK E  — Weight diagnostics: DML vs kappa (all 4 DML fits)
#   BLOCK F  — Love plots: DML vs kappa (Card spec, somecol; most illustrative)
#   BLOCK G  — Summary table: DML vs kappa point estimates (dollars)
# ==============================================================================


# ==============================================================================
# BLOCK A — SETUP
# Run first. Requires card_1995_replication.R objects to be in memory.
# ==============================================================================
#
# Objects expected from card_1995_replication.R:
#   card_clean  : cleaned Card (1995) data frame
#   Z           : instrument vector (nearc4)
#   D1          : somecol = 1{educ > 12}
#   D2          : educ16  = 1{educ >= 16}
#   Y_dol       : log(wage/100) — dollars before log
#   Y_cnt       : log(wage)     — cents before log
#   X_card_kappa: Card spec design matrix WITH intercept
#   X_kit_kappa : Kitagawa spec design matrix WITH intercept
#   X_card_df   : Card spec data.frame WITHOUT intercept (for 2SLS)
#   X_kit_df    : Kitagawa spec data.frame WITHOUT intercept
#
# Toolkit functions expected (from card_1995_replication.R §3a–§3v):
#   safe_logit(), prep_design_for_mest(), logit_mle(), fit_logit_alpha(),
#   fit_cbps_alpha(), cbps(), get_cbps_p(), kappa_weights(),
#   tau_u(), tau_a10(), tau_unnorm(), kappa_outcome_weights(),
#   kappa_weights_bundle(), num_jacobian(), matrix_inverse_safe(),
#   sandwich_se_mest(), alpha_moment_matrix(), kappa_analytic_se_one(),
#   safe_kappa_se(), kappa_analytic_se_all(), run_2sls(),
#   weight_diag(), check_weight_identity(), fmt()

library(OutcomeWeights)  # dml_with_smoother(), get_outcome_weights()
library(cobalt)          # love.plot()
##  cobalt (Version 4.6.2, Build Date: 2026-01-29)
library(viridis)         # colour palette for Love plots
## Loading required package: viridisLite
library(gridExtra)       # grid.arrange()
library(ggplot2)         # required by OutcomeWeights

# Guard: verify that card_1995_replication.R has been run
stopifnot(
  exists("card_clean"), exists("D1"), exists("D2"), exists("Z"),
  exists("Y_dol"), exists("Y_cnt"),
  exists("kappa_weights"), exists("tau_u"), exists("tau_a10"),
  exists("tau_unnorm"), exists("logit_mle"), exists("kappa_outcome_weights"),
  exists("weight_diag"),
  exists("check_weight_identity"), exists("get_cbps_p")
)

k_shift <- log(100)  # Y_cnt = Y_dol + k_shift (used in Block D)

cat(sprintf("BLOCK A ready. N=%d | mean(D1)=%.3f | mean(D2)=%.3f | mean(Z)=%.3f\n\n",
            nrow(card_clean), mean(D1), mean(D2), mean(Z)))
## BLOCK A ready. N=3010 | mean(D1)=0.505 | mean(D2)=0.271 | mean(Z)=0.682
# ==============================================================================
# BLOCK A: X matrices for DML
# ==============================================================================
# DML requires a CLEAN numeric matrix without factor attributes.
# grf::validate_X() rejects matrices with lingering factor-level attributes.
# make_X() strips all attributes while preserving column names.
# ncol >= 2 required — single-column matrices fail grf in R 4.5.x.
#
# Card specification for DML (same variables as X_card_kappa, minus intercept):
#   exper, expersq, black, south, smsa, smsa66, reg661–reg668
#   This is a 14-column matrix.
#
# Kitagawa specification for DML (same variables as X_kit_kappa, minus intercept):
#   black, south, smsa, smsa66, south66
#   This is a 5-column matrix.
#
# IMPORTANT: DML X matrices have NO intercept (grf adds it internally).
#            Kappa X matrices (X_card_kappa, X_kit_kappa) retain the intercept
#            column because logit_mle() and cbps() need it.

make_X <- function(formula, data) {
  # Strips all attributes from model.matrix output to produce a plain numeric
  # matrix safe for grf::validate_X(). This is identical to the Vietnam DML
  # implementation (vietmam_14_05_double_ml.R, Block A).
  mm <- model.matrix(formula, data = data)
  m  <- matrix(as.numeric(mm), nrow = nrow(mm), ncol = ncol(mm))
  colnames(m) <- colnames(mm)
  m
}

# Card spec DML matrix (14 covariates, no intercept)
X_dml_card <- make_X(
  ~ 0 + exper + expersq + black + south + smsa + smsa66 +
    reg661 + reg662 + reg663 + reg664 + reg665 + reg666 + reg667 + reg668,
  data = card_clean
)

# Kitagawa spec DML matrix (5 covariates, no intercept)
X_dml_kit <- make_X(
  ~ 0 + black + south + smsa + smsa66 + south66,
  data = card_clean
)

cat("DML X matrices:\n")
## DML X matrices:
cat(sprintf("  X_dml_card: %dx%d | numeric=%s\n",
            nrow(X_dml_card), ncol(X_dml_card), is.numeric(X_dml_card)))
##   X_dml_card: 3010x14 | numeric=TRUE
cat(sprintf("  X_dml_kit:  %dx%d | numeric=%s\n",
            nrow(X_dml_kit), ncol(X_dml_kit), is.numeric(X_dml_kit)))
##   X_dml_kit:  3010x5 | numeric=TRUE
# Helper functions specific to DML extraction
# (identical in purpose to Vietnam DML Block A; kept here for self-containedness)

get_estimate <- function(res, row_name) {
  # Extract point estimate from OutcomeWeights summary() output.
  # Handles both "Estimate" column name and positional access.
  if ("Estimate" %in% colnames(res)) return(as.numeric(res[row_name, "Estimate"]))
  as.numeric(res[row_name, 1])
}

check_omega_rows <- function(omega_obj, rows = c("PLR-IV", "Wald-AIPW")) {
  # Verify that the expected outcome-weight rows exist in the omega object.
  missing_rows <- setdiff(rows, rownames(omega_obj$omega))
  if (length(missing_rows) > 0)
    stop("Missing expected outcome-weight rows: ", paste(missing_rows, collapse = ", "))
}

cat("\nBLOCK A complete. Ready for BLOCKS B and C.\n\n")
## 
## BLOCK A complete. Ready for BLOCKS B and C.
# ==============================================================================
# BLOCK B — DML: CARD SPECIFICATION
# X = [exper, expersq, black, south, smsa, smsa66, reg661–reg668]
# Corresponds to columns 1-2 and 5-6 of SUW Table 3.
# ==============================================================================
#
# This is the richer, more policy-relevant specification. With 14 covariates
# (binary region indicators + continuous experience), a random forest can learn
# interactions that a linear propensity model cannot capture. The comparison
# between DML and kappa estimates here is the main substantive result of
# Chapter 5 for the Card application.
#
# Both treatments (somecol = D1, educ16 = D2) are estimated on log wages in
# dollars (Y_dol). The same X_dml_card matrix is used for both.
#
# n_cf_folds = 5 : 5-fold cross-fitting (standard in the DML literature)
# tune.parameters = "all" : hyperparameter tuning for the grf random forest
#   (matches Vietnam DML Block B; tuning substantially improves balance there)
# set.seed(42) : reproducibility

set.seed(42)
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## ======================================================================
cat("BLOCK B1 — DML: Card specification, somecol (D1 = educ > 12)\n")
## BLOCK B1 — DML: Card specification, somecol (D1 = educ > 12)
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n\n")
## ======================================================================
# --- B1: somecol, Card spec ---
# Treatment: D1 = somecol = 1{educ > 12}
# Outcome:   Y_dol = log(wage/100)   (dollars)
# Instrument: Z = nearc4
# Covariates: X_dml_card (Card specification, no intercept)

dml_card_d1 <- dml_with_smoother(Y_dol, D1, X_dml_card, Z,
                                  n_cf_folds = 5, tune.parameters = "all")
res_card_d1 <- summary(dml_card_d1)
##           Estimate       SE       t       p    
## PLR       0.239084 0.017647 13.5482 < 2e-16 ***
## PLR-IV    0.680717 0.288671  2.3581 0.01843 *  
## AIPW-ATE       NaN       NA     NaN     NaN    
## Wald-AIPW 0.279284 0.214392  1.3027 0.19278    
## AIPW-ATT       NaN       NA     NaN     NaN    
## AIPW-ATU  0.227007 0.019826 11.4501 < 2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
cat("Full summary:\n")
## Full summary:
print(res_card_d1)
##            Estimate         SE         t            p
## PLR       0.2390836 0.01764689 13.548195 1.231501e-40
## PLR-IV    0.6807170 0.28867077  2.358109 1.843194e-02
## AIPW-ATE        NaN         NA       NaN          NaN
## Wald-AIPW 0.2792842 0.21439178  1.302682 1.927831e-01
## AIPW-ATT        NaN         NA       NaN          NaN
## AIPW-ATU  0.2270071 0.01982575 11.450113 9.606545e-30
cat("\nIV-relevant rows (PLR-IV, Wald-AIPW):\n")
## 
## IV-relevant rows (PLR-IV, Wald-AIPW):
print(res_card_d1[c("PLR-IV", "Wald-AIPW"), , drop = FALSE])
##            Estimate        SE        t          p
## PLR-IV    0.6807170 0.2886708 2.358109 0.01843194
## Wald-AIPW 0.2792842 0.2143918 1.302682 0.19278313
# Extract outcome weights
# omega$omega is a (rows x N) matrix; rows = PLR / PLR-IV / AIPW-ATE / Wald-AIPW
omega_card_d1       <- get_outcome_weights(dml_card_d1)
check_omega_rows(omega_card_d1)
w_plriv_card_d1     <- as.vector(omega_card_d1$omega["PLR-IV",    ])
w_waldaipw_card_d1  <- as.vector(omega_card_d1$omega["Wald-AIPW", ])

# Algebraic identity: omega'Y = tau_hat must hold exactly (Knaus 2024)
cat("\nAlgebraic check (omega'Y = tau_hat, must be TRUE):\n")
## 
## Algebraic check (omega'Y = tau_hat, must be TRUE):
cat("  PLR-IV:   ",
    check_weight_identity(w_plriv_card_d1,    Y_dol, get_estimate(res_card_d1, "PLR-IV")), "\n")
##   PLR-IV:    TRUE
cat("  Wald-AIPW:",
    check_weight_identity(w_waldaipw_card_d1, Y_dol, get_estimate(res_card_d1, "Wald-AIPW")), "\n\n")
##   Wald-AIPW: TRUE
cat("Weight diagnostics (Sum_w = 0 => translation invariant):\n")
## Weight diagnostics (Sum_w = 0 => translation invariant):
print(rbind(
  weight_diag(w_plriv_card_d1,    "PLR-IV     (DML, Card, somecol)"),
  weight_diag(w_waldaipw_card_d1, "Wald-AIPW  (DML, Card, somecol)")
), row.names = FALSE)
##                        Estimator Sum_w ESS Pct_neg Max_abs_w
##  PLR-IV     (DML, Card, somecol)     0   2    33.1  0.032653
##  Wald-AIPW  (DML, Card, somecol)     0   3    31.8  0.145639
cat("\nBLOCK B1 done.\n\n")
## 
## BLOCK B1 done.
# --- B2: educ16, Card spec ---
set.seed(42)
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## ======================================================================
cat("BLOCK B2 — DML: Card specification, educ16 (D2 = educ >= 16)\n")
## BLOCK B2 — DML: Card specification, educ16 (D2 = educ >= 16)
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n\n")
## ======================================================================
# Treatment: D2 = educ16 = 1{educ >= 16}
# Outcome:   Y_dol = log(wage/100)   (dollars)
# Instrument: Z = nearc4
# Covariates: X_dml_card (Card specification, no intercept)
# Note: The same DML machinery is applied; only D changes.

dml_card_d2 <- dml_with_smoother(Y_dol, D2, X_dml_card, Z,
                                  n_cf_folds = 5, tune.parameters = "all")
res_card_d2 <- summary(dml_card_d2)
##           Estimate       SE       t       p    
## PLR       0.319529 0.018839 16.9612 < 2e-16 ***
## PLR-IV    1.139922 0.535281  2.1296 0.03329 *  
## AIPW-ATE  0.342322 0.015054 22.7398 < 2e-16 ***
## Wald-AIPW 0.530289 0.397728  1.3333 0.18254    
## AIPW-ATT  0.238301 0.019234 12.3897 < 2e-16 ***
## AIPW-ATU  0.381075 0.015771 24.1637 < 2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
cat("Full summary:\n")
## Full summary:
print(res_card_d2)
##            Estimate         SE         t             p
## PLR       0.3195286 0.01883882 16.961180  1.069587e-61
## PLR-IV    1.1399217 0.53528057  2.129578  3.328740e-02
## AIPW-ATE  0.3423222 0.01505385 22.739846 9.066224e-106
## Wald-AIPW 0.5302893 0.39772777  1.333297  1.825352e-01
## AIPW-ATT  0.2383014 0.01923378 12.389736  2.021585e-34
## AIPW-ATU  0.3810751 0.01577059 24.163655 4.736953e-118
cat("\nIV-relevant rows (PLR-IV, Wald-AIPW):\n")
## 
## IV-relevant rows (PLR-IV, Wald-AIPW):
print(res_card_d2[c("PLR-IV", "Wald-AIPW"), , drop = FALSE])
##            Estimate        SE        t         p
## PLR-IV    1.1399217 0.5352806 2.129578 0.0332874
## Wald-AIPW 0.5302893 0.3977278 1.333297 0.1825352
omega_card_d2       <- get_outcome_weights(dml_card_d2)
check_omega_rows(omega_card_d2)
w_plriv_card_d2     <- as.vector(omega_card_d2$omega["PLR-IV",    ])
w_waldaipw_card_d2  <- as.vector(omega_card_d2$omega["Wald-AIPW", ])

cat("\nAlgebraic check (omega'Y = tau_hat, must be TRUE):\n")
## 
## Algebraic check (omega'Y = tau_hat, must be TRUE):
cat("  PLR-IV:   ",
    check_weight_identity(w_plriv_card_d2,    Y_dol, get_estimate(res_card_d2, "PLR-IV")), "\n")
##   PLR-IV:    TRUE
cat("  Wald-AIPW:",
    check_weight_identity(w_waldaipw_card_d2, Y_dol, get_estimate(res_card_d2, "Wald-AIPW")), "\n\n")
##   Wald-AIPW: TRUE
cat("Weight diagnostics:\n")
## Weight diagnostics:
print(rbind(
  weight_diag(w_plriv_card_d2,    "PLR-IV     (DML, Card, educ16)"),
  weight_diag(w_waldaipw_card_d2, "Wald-AIPW  (DML, Card, educ16)")
), row.names = FALSE)
##                       Estimator Sum_w ESS Pct_neg Max_abs_w
##  PLR-IV     (DML, Card, educ16)     0   1    33.1  0.054681
##  Wald-AIPW  (DML, Card, educ16)     0   1    31.8  0.276531
cat("\nBLOCK B2 done.\n\n")
## 
## BLOCK B2 done.
# ==============================================================================
# BLOCK C — DML: KITAGAWA SPECIFICATION
# X = [black, south, smsa, smsa66, south66]  (5 covariates, no intercept)
# Corresponds to columns 3-4 and 7-8 of SUW Table 3.
# ==============================================================================
#
# The Kitagawa spec is parsimonious — only 5 binary variables. In this low-
# dimensional setting, random forests have less to learn, and the DML and kappa
# estimates should converge more than in the Card spec. This comparison is
# informative: when the covariate space is small, ML flexibility is not needed
# and the methods agree; when it is large (Block B), they can diverge.
#
# NOTE: Both treatments are estimated as in Block B.

set.seed(42)
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## ======================================================================
cat("BLOCK C1 — DML: Kitagawa specification, somecol (D1 = educ > 12)\n")
## BLOCK C1 — DML: Kitagawa specification, somecol (D1 = educ > 12)
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n\n")
## ======================================================================
# --- C1: somecol, Kitagawa spec ---
# Treatment: D1 = somecol
# Outcome:   Y_dol
# Instrument: Z = nearc4
# Covariates: X_dml_kit (Kitagawa specification, no intercept)

dml_kit_d1 <- dml_with_smoother(Y_dol, D1, X_dml_kit, Z,
                                  n_cf_folds = 5, tune.parameters = "all")
res_kit_d1 <- summary(dml_kit_d1)
##           Estimate       SE      t         p    
## PLR       0.112698 0.015081 7.4729 1.022e-13 ***
## PLR-IV    0.592544 0.340076 1.7424   0.08154 .  
## AIPW-ATE  0.116735 0.015018 7.7730 1.044e-14 ***
## Wald-AIPW 0.292078 0.248921 1.1734   0.24074    
## AIPW-ATT  0.108982 0.015535 7.0154 2.820e-12 ***
## AIPW-ATU  0.124655 0.015908 7.8359 6.405e-15 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
cat("Full summary:\n")
## Full summary:
print(res_kit_d1)
##            Estimate         SE        t            p
## PLR       0.1126977 0.01508079 7.472933 1.022218e-13
## PLR-IV    0.5925436 0.34007622 1.742385 8.154326e-02
## AIPW-ATE  0.1167353 0.01501806 7.772995 1.044111e-14
## Wald-AIPW 0.2920781 0.24892112 1.173376 2.407378e-01
## AIPW-ATT  0.1089823 0.01553466 7.015433 2.819743e-12
## AIPW-ATU  0.1246549 0.01590824 7.835874 6.405138e-15
cat("\nIV-relevant rows (PLR-IV, Wald-AIPW):\n")
## 
## IV-relevant rows (PLR-IV, Wald-AIPW):
print(res_kit_d1[c("PLR-IV", "Wald-AIPW"), , drop = FALSE])
##            Estimate        SE        t          p
## PLR-IV    0.5925436 0.3400762 1.742385 0.08154326
## Wald-AIPW 0.2920781 0.2489211 1.173376 0.24073779
omega_kit_d1       <- get_outcome_weights(dml_kit_d1)
check_omega_rows(omega_kit_d1)
w_plriv_kit_d1     <- as.vector(omega_kit_d1$omega["PLR-IV",    ])
w_waldaipw_kit_d1  <- as.vector(omega_kit_d1$omega["Wald-AIPW", ])

cat("\nAlgebraic check (omega'Y = tau_hat, must be TRUE):\n")
## 
## Algebraic check (omega'Y = tau_hat, must be TRUE):
cat("  PLR-IV:   ",
    check_weight_identity(w_plriv_kit_d1,    Y_dol, get_estimate(res_kit_d1, "PLR-IV")), "\n")
##   PLR-IV:    TRUE
cat("  Wald-AIPW:",
    check_weight_identity(w_waldaipw_kit_d1, Y_dol, get_estimate(res_kit_d1, "Wald-AIPW")), "\n\n")
##   Wald-AIPW: TRUE
cat("Weight diagnostics (Sum_w = 0 => translation invariant):\n")
## Weight diagnostics (Sum_w = 0 => translation invariant):
print(rbind(
  weight_diag(w_plriv_kit_d1,    "PLR-IV     (DML, Kitagawa, somecol)"),
  weight_diag(w_waldaipw_kit_d1, "Wald-AIPW  (DML, Kitagawa, somecol)")
), row.names = FALSE)
##                            Estimator Sum_w ESS Pct_neg Max_abs_w
##  PLR-IV     (DML, Kitagawa, somecol)     0   2    31.8  0.032775
##  Wald-AIPW  (DML, Kitagawa, somecol)     0   3    31.8  0.065150
cat("\nBLOCK C1 done.\n\n")
## 
## BLOCK C1 done.
# --- C2: educ16, Kitagawa spec ---
set.seed(42)
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## ======================================================================
cat("BLOCK C2 — DML: Kitagawa specification, educ16 (D2 = educ >= 16)\n")
## BLOCK C2 — DML: Kitagawa specification, educ16 (D2 = educ >= 16)
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n\n")
## ======================================================================
dml_kit_d2 <- dml_with_smoother(Y_dol, D2, X_dml_kit, Z,
                                  n_cf_folds = 5, tune.parameters = "all")
res_kit_d2 <- summary(dml_kit_d2)
##           Estimate       SE      t         p    
## PLR       0.152182 0.017650 8.6220 < 2.2e-16 ***
## PLR-IV    1.030285 0.683563 1.5072    0.1319    
## AIPW-ATE  0.172489 0.017927 9.6218 < 2.2e-16 ***
## Wald-AIPW 0.534740 0.462037 1.1574    0.2472    
## AIPW-ATT  0.147088 0.017984 8.1789 4.175e-16 ***
## AIPW-ATU  0.181952 0.018986 9.5834 < 2.2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
cat("Full summary:\n")
## Full summary:
print(res_kit_d2)
##            Estimate         SE        t            p
## PLR       0.1521825 0.01765040 8.622040 1.045737e-17
## PLR-IV    1.0302846 0.68356266 1.507228 1.318572e-01
## AIPW-ATE  0.1724893 0.01792687 9.621831 1.318902e-21
## Wald-AIPW 0.5347398 0.46203667 1.157354 2.472198e-01
## AIPW-ATT  0.1470882 0.01798376 8.178947 4.175214e-16
## AIPW-ATU  0.1819525 0.01898621 9.583403 1.893906e-21
cat("\nIV-relevant rows (PLR-IV, Wald-AIPW):\n")
## 
## IV-relevant rows (PLR-IV, Wald-AIPW):
print(res_kit_d2[c("PLR-IV", "Wald-AIPW"), , drop = FALSE])
##            Estimate        SE        t         p
## PLR-IV    1.0302846 0.6835627 1.507228 0.1318572
## Wald-AIPW 0.5347398 0.4620367 1.157354 0.2472198
omega_kit_d2       <- get_outcome_weights(dml_kit_d2)
check_omega_rows(omega_kit_d2)
w_plriv_kit_d2     <- as.vector(omega_kit_d2$omega["PLR-IV",    ])
w_waldaipw_kit_d2  <- as.vector(omega_kit_d2$omega["Wald-AIPW", ])

cat("\nAlgebraic check (omega'Y = tau_hat, must be TRUE):\n")
## 
## Algebraic check (omega'Y = tau_hat, must be TRUE):
cat("  PLR-IV:   ",
    check_weight_identity(w_plriv_kit_d2,    Y_dol, get_estimate(res_kit_d2, "PLR-IV")), "\n")
##   PLR-IV:    TRUE
cat("  Wald-AIPW:",
    check_weight_identity(w_waldaipw_kit_d2, Y_dol, get_estimate(res_kit_d2, "Wald-AIPW")), "\n\n")
##   Wald-AIPW: TRUE
cat("Weight diagnostics:\n")
## Weight diagnostics:
print(rbind(
  weight_diag(w_plriv_kit_d2,    "PLR-IV     (DML, Kitagawa, educ16)"),
  weight_diag(w_waldaipw_kit_d2, "Wald-AIPW  (DML, Kitagawa, educ16)")
), row.names = FALSE)
##                           Estimator Sum_w ESS Pct_neg Max_abs_w
##  PLR-IV     (DML, Kitagawa, educ16)     0   1    31.8  0.056988
##  Wald-AIPW  (DML, Kitagawa, educ16)     0   1    31.8  0.119277
cat("\nBLOCK C2 done.\n\n")
## 
## BLOCK C2 done.
# ==============================================================================
# BLOCK D — TRANSLATION INVARIANCE: DML VS KAPPA (all 4 DML fits)
# Requires: BLOCKS B and C.
# ==============================================================================
#
# For any estimator tau_hat = sum_i omega_i Y_i:
#
#   tau_hat(Y_cnt) - tau_hat(Y_dol) = log(100) * sum_i omega_i
#
# because Y_cnt = Y_dol + log(100). Therefore sum(omega_i) = 0 is the direct
# empirical diagnostic for translation invariance. DML (PLR-IV and Wald-AIPW)
# satisfies this by construction; the unnormalized kappa estimators do not.
#
# This block reproduces the translation-invariance table from the kappa
# replication (Section 7 of card_1995_replication.R), now augmented with the
# DML weight vectors. Because DML is run only on Y_dol, we compute the kappa
# estimates on both Y_cnt and Y_dol at this step.

cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## ======================================================================
cat("BLOCK D — TRANSLATION INVARIANCE: DML VS KAPPA\n")
## BLOCK D — TRANSLATION INVARIANCE: DML VS KAPPA
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## ======================================================================
cat(sprintf("k = log(100) = %.6f\n\n", k_shift))
## k = log(100) = 4.605170
# Helper: one row of the translation-invariance table
# (Adapted from kappa replication; now accepts any weight vector generically.)
print_inv_table_dml <- function(spec_label, D,
                                 w_plriv, w_waldaipw,
                                 X_kappa, Y_dol, Y_cnt) {
  cat(sprintf("\n--- %s ---\n", spec_label))
  cat(sprintf("  %-26s  %9s  %9s  %10s  %10s  %7s\n",
              "Estimator", "Dollars", "Cents", "Diff(act)", "Diff(pred)", "Match?"))
  cat(strrep("-", 80), "\n")

  p_ml  <- logit_mle(Z, X_kappa)
  p_cb  <- get_cbps_p(Z, X_kappa)
  kw_ml <- kappa_outcome_weights(Z, D, p_ml)
  kw_cb <- kappa_outcome_weights(Z, D, p_cb)

  rows <- list(
    list(name = "PLR-IV (DML)",
         w = w_plriv,
         ed = sum(w_plriv * Y_dol),
         ec = sum(w_plriv * Y_cnt)),
    list(name = "Wald-AIPW (DML)",
         w = w_waldaipw,
         ed = sum(w_waldaipw * Y_dol),
         ec = sum(w_waldaipw * Y_cnt)),
    list(name = "tau_cb_u",
         w = kw_cb$w_u,
         ed = tau_u(Y_dol, Z, D, p_cb),
         ec = tau_u(Y_cnt, Z, D, p_cb)),
    list(name = "tau_ml_u",
         w = kw_ml$w_u,
         ed = tau_u(Y_dol, Z, D, p_ml),
         ec = tau_u(Y_cnt, Z, D, p_ml)),
    list(name = "tau_ml_a10",
         w = kw_ml$w_a10,
         ed = tau_a10(Y_dol, Z, D, p_ml),
         ec = tau_a10(Y_cnt, Z, D, p_ml)),
    list(name = "tau_ml_a",
         w = kw_ml$w_a,
         ed = tau_unnorm(Y_dol, Z, D, p_ml, "a"),
         ec = tau_unnorm(Y_cnt, Z, D, p_ml, "a")),
    list(name = "tau_ml_a1/t",
         w = kw_ml$w_a1,
         ed = tau_unnorm(Y_dol, Z, D, p_ml, "a1"),
         ec = tau_unnorm(Y_cnt, Z, D, p_ml, "a1")),
    list(name = "tau_ml_a0",
         w = kw_ml$w_a0,
         ed = tau_unnorm(Y_dol, Z, D, p_ml, "a0"),
         ec = tau_unnorm(Y_cnt, Z, D, p_ml, "a0"))
  )

  for (r in rows) {
    act  <- r$ec - r$ed
    pred <- sum(r$w) * k_shift
    ok   <- isTRUE(all.equal(act, pred, tolerance = 1e-6))
    cat(sprintf("  %-26s  %9.4f  %9.4f  %10.4f  %10.4f  %7s\n",
                r$name, r$ed, r$ec, act, pred,
                if (ok) "TRUE" else "FALSE"))
  }
}

print_inv_table_dml("Card spec — somecol (D1)",
                    D1, w_plriv_card_d1, w_waldaipw_card_d1,
                    X_card_kappa, Y_dol, Y_cnt)
## 
## --- Card spec — somecol (D1) ---
##   Estimator                     Dollars      Cents   Diff(act)  Diff(pred)   Match?
## -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 
##   PLR-IV (DML)                   0.6807     0.6807      0.0000      0.0000     TRUE
##   Wald-AIPW (DML)                0.2793     0.2793      0.0000     -0.0000     TRUE
##   tau_cb_u                       0.3762     0.3762     -0.0000     -0.0000     TRUE
##   tau_ml_u                       0.3308     0.3308     -0.0000     -0.0000     TRUE
##   tau_ml_a10                     0.3460     0.3460      0.0000      0.0000     TRUE
##   tau_ml_a                       0.1695    -0.3193     -0.4888     -0.4888     TRUE
##   tau_ml_a1/t                    0.1707    -0.3214     -0.4921     -0.4921     TRUE
##   tau_ml_a0                      0.1542    -0.2904     -0.4446     -0.4446     TRUE
print_inv_table_dml("Card spec — educ16 (D2)",
                    D2, w_plriv_card_d2, w_waldaipw_card_d2,
                    X_card_kappa, Y_dol, Y_cnt)
## 
## --- Card spec — educ16 (D2) ---
##   Estimator                     Dollars      Cents   Diff(act)  Diff(pred)   Match?
## -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 
##   PLR-IV (DML)                   1.1399     1.1399     -0.0000      0.0000     TRUE
##   Wald-AIPW (DML)                0.5303     0.5303     -0.0000     -0.0000     TRUE
##   tau_cb_u                       0.8530     0.8530     -0.0000     -0.0000     TRUE
##   tau_ml_u                       0.6191     0.6191     -0.0000     -0.0000     TRUE
##   tau_ml_a10                     0.5862     0.5862     -0.0000     -0.0000     TRUE
##   tau_ml_a                       0.3154    -0.5939     -0.9093     -0.9093     TRUE
##   tau_ml_a1/t                    0.3193    -0.6013     -0.9206     -0.9206     TRUE
##   tau_ml_a0                      0.2661    -0.5012     -0.7673     -0.7673     TRUE
print_inv_table_dml("Kitagawa spec — somecol (D1)",
                    D1, w_plriv_kit_d1, w_waldaipw_kit_d1,
                    X_kit_kappa, Y_dol, Y_cnt)
## 
## --- Kitagawa spec — somecol (D1) ---
##   Estimator                     Dollars      Cents   Diff(act)  Diff(pred)   Match?
## -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 
##   PLR-IV (DML)                   0.5925     0.5925      0.0000      0.0000     TRUE
##   Wald-AIPW (DML)                0.2921     0.2921      0.0000      0.0000     TRUE
##   tau_cb_u                       0.3310     0.3310      0.0000      0.0000     TRUE
##   tau_ml_u                       0.3556     0.3556      0.0000      0.0000     TRUE
##   tau_ml_a10                     0.2930     0.2930      0.0000     -0.0000     TRUE
##   tau_ml_a                       0.8418     2.2476      1.4058      1.4058     TRUE
##   tau_ml_a1/t                    0.7688     2.0526      1.2838      1.2838     TRUE
##   tau_ml_a0                      1.0659     2.8460      1.7801      1.7801     TRUE
print_inv_table_dml("Kitagawa spec — educ16 (D2)",
                    D2, w_plriv_kit_d2, w_waldaipw_kit_d2,
                    X_kit_kappa, Y_dol, Y_cnt)
## 
## --- Kitagawa spec — educ16 (D2) ---
##   Estimator                     Dollars      Cents   Diff(act)  Diff(pred)   Match?
## -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 
##   PLR-IV (DML)                   1.0303     1.0303      0.0000      0.0000     TRUE
##   Wald-AIPW (DML)                0.5347     0.5347      0.0000      0.0000     TRUE
##   tau_cb_u                       0.5885     0.5885      0.0000      0.0000     TRUE
##   tau_ml_u                       0.6276     0.6276      0.0000      0.0000     TRUE
##   tau_ml_a10                     0.8356     0.8356      0.0000     -0.0000     TRUE
##   tau_ml_a                       1.6168     4.3169      2.7001      2.7001     TRUE
##   tau_ml_a1/t                    1.3673     3.6507      2.2834      2.2834     TRUE
##   tau_ml_a0                      2.7120     7.2410      4.5290      4.5290     TRUE
cat("\nInterpretation:\n")
## 
## Interpretation:
cat("  Sum_w ~= 0  => translation invariant (DML, tau_u, tau_a10).\n")
##   Sum_w ~= 0  => translation invariant (DML, tau_u, tau_a10).
cat("  Sum_w != 0  => estimate changes with outcome unit (unnormalized kappa).\n\n")
##   Sum_w != 0  => estimate changes with outcome unit (unnormalized kappa).
# ==============================================================================
# BLOCK E — WEIGHT DIAGNOSTICS: DML VS KAPPA (all 4 DML fits)
# Requires: BLOCKS B, C, and the kappa_weights_bundle() function.
# ==============================================================================
#
# Compares Sum_w, ESS, % negative weights, and max|omega| for DML and kappa
# estimators across all (treatment, spec) combinations. Key questions:
#   - Is the ESS similar across estimators? (If so, differences are driven by
#     normalization, not by which observations are effectively up-weighted.)
#   - Does the Card spec (more covariates) produce lower ESS than Kitagawa?
#     Lower ESS => some observations dominate, which can inflate variance.
#   - Do the unnormalized kappa estimators have non-zero Sum_w? (They should.)

cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## ======================================================================
cat("BLOCK E — WEIGHT DIAGNOSTICS: DML VS KAPPA\n")
## BLOCK E — WEIGHT DIAGNOSTICS: DML VS KAPPA
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n\n")
## ======================================================================
cat("Sum_w = 0 => translation invariant | ESS = 1 / sum(w_i^2)\n\n")
## Sum_w = 0 => translation invariant | ESS = 1 / sum(w_i^2)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Guard: re-enforce the toolkit's 3-argument kappa_weights_bundle signature.
# The Vietnam DML script defines a 1-argument closure version in its Block A
# (kappa_weights_bundle(X_kappa) with Z and D captured from the environment).
# If that session is still active, it overwrites the toolkit version and causes
# "unused arguments (D1, X_card_kappa)". We always redefine explicitly here.
# This is identical in logic to the toolkit §5, just made explicit for safety.
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
kappa_weights_bundle <- function(Z, D, X_kappa) {
  p_ml  <- logit_mle(Z, X_kappa)
  p_cb  <- get_cbps_p(Z, X_kappa)
  kw_ml <- kappa_outcome_weights(Z, D, p_ml)
  kw_cb <- kappa_outcome_weights(Z, D, p_cb)
  list(
    tau_cb_u   = kw_cb$w_u,
    tau_ml_u   = kw_ml$w_u,
    tau_ml_a10 = kw_ml$w_a10,
    tau_ml_a   = kw_ml$w_a,
    tau_ml_a1  = kw_ml$w_a1,
    tau_ml_a0  = kw_ml$w_a0
  )
}

# --- E1: somecol, Card spec ---
cat("--- Card specification, somecol (D1) ---\n")
## --- Card specification, somecol (D1) ---
kw_card_d1 <- kappa_weights_bundle(Z, D1, X_card_kappa)

diag_card_d1 <- rbind(
  weight_diag(w_plriv_card_d1,       "PLR-IV        (DML, Card, somecol)"),
  weight_diag(w_waldaipw_card_d1,    "Wald-AIPW     (DML, Card, somecol)"),
  weight_diag(kw_card_d1$tau_cb_u,   "tau_cb_u      (kappa, Card, somecol)"),
  weight_diag(kw_card_d1$tau_ml_u,   "tau_ml_u      (kappa, Card, somecol)"),
  weight_diag(kw_card_d1$tau_ml_a10, "tau_ml_a10    (kappa, Card, somecol)"),
  weight_diag(kw_card_d1$tau_ml_a,   "tau_ml_a      (kappa, Card, somecol)"),
  weight_diag(kw_card_d1$tau_ml_a1,  "tau_ml_a1/t   (kappa, Card, somecol)"),
  weight_diag(kw_card_d1$tau_ml_a0,  "tau_ml_a0     (kappa, Card, somecol)")
)
print(diag_card_d1, row.names = FALSE)
##                             Estimator       Sum_w ESS Pct_neg Max_abs_w
##    PLR-IV        (DML, Card, somecol)  0.00000000   2    33.1  0.032653
##    Wald-AIPW     (DML, Card, somecol)  0.00000000   3    31.8  0.145639
##  tau_cb_u      (kappa, Card, somecol)  0.00000000   3    31.8  0.086905
##  tau_ml_u      (kappa, Card, somecol)  0.00000000   4    31.8  0.051920
##  tau_ml_a10    (kappa, Card, somecol)  0.00000000   4    31.8  0.054816
##  tau_ml_a      (kappa, Card, somecol) -0.10615006   3    31.8  0.054452
##  tau_ml_a1/t   (kappa, Card, somecol) -0.10685947   3    31.8  0.054816
##  tau_ml_a0     (kappa, Card, somecol) -0.09654294   4    31.8  0.049524
# --- E2: educ16, Card spec ---
cat("\n--- Card specification, educ16 (D2) ---\n")
## 
## --- Card specification, educ16 (D2) ---
kw_card_d2 <- kappa_weights_bundle(Z, D2, X_card_kappa)

diag_card_d2 <- rbind(
  weight_diag(w_plriv_card_d2,       "PLR-IV        (DML, Card, educ16)"),
  weight_diag(w_waldaipw_card_d2,    "Wald-AIPW     (DML, Card, educ16)"),
  weight_diag(kw_card_d2$tau_cb_u,   "tau_cb_u      (kappa, Card, educ16)"),
  weight_diag(kw_card_d2$tau_ml_u,   "tau_ml_u      (kappa, Card, educ16)"),
  weight_diag(kw_card_d2$tau_ml_a10, "tau_ml_a10    (kappa, Card, educ16)"),
  weight_diag(kw_card_d2$tau_ml_a,   "tau_ml_a      (kappa, Card, educ16)"),
  weight_diag(kw_card_d2$tau_ml_a1,  "tau_ml_a1/t   (kappa, Card, educ16)"),
  weight_diag(kw_card_d2$tau_ml_a0,  "tau_ml_a0     (kappa, Card, educ16)")
)
print(diag_card_d2, row.names = FALSE)
##                            Estimator      Sum_w ESS Pct_neg Max_abs_w
##    PLR-IV        (DML, Card, educ16)  0.0000000   1    33.1  0.054681
##    Wald-AIPW     (DML, Card, educ16)  0.0000000   1    31.8  0.276531
##  tau_cb_u      (kappa, Card, educ16)  0.0000000   1    31.8  0.197058
##  tau_ml_u      (kappa, Card, educ16)  0.0000000   1    31.8  0.097168
##  tau_ml_a10    (kappa, Card, educ16)  0.0000000   1    31.8  0.102551
##  tau_ml_a      (kappa, Card, educ16) -0.1974461   1    31.8  0.101285
##  tau_ml_a1/t   (kappa, Card, educ16) -0.1999148   1    31.8  0.102551
##  tau_ml_a0     (kappa, Card, educ16) -0.1666075   1    31.8  0.085466
# --- E3: somecol, Kitagawa spec ---
cat("\n--- Kitagawa specification, somecol (D1) ---\n")
## 
## --- Kitagawa specification, somecol (D1) ---
kw_kit_d1 <- kappa_weights_bundle(Z, D1, X_kit_kappa)

diag_kit_d1 <- rbind(
  weight_diag(w_plriv_kit_d1,       "PLR-IV        (DML, Kitagawa, somecol)"),
  weight_diag(w_waldaipw_kit_d1,    "Wald-AIPW     (DML, Kitagawa, somecol)"),
  weight_diag(kw_kit_d1$tau_cb_u,   "tau_cb_u      (kappa, Kitagawa, somecol)"),
  weight_diag(kw_kit_d1$tau_ml_u,   "tau_ml_u      (kappa, Kitagawa, somecol)"),
  weight_diag(kw_kit_d1$tau_ml_a10, "tau_ml_a10    (kappa, Kitagawa, somecol)"),
  weight_diag(kw_kit_d1$tau_ml_a,   "tau_ml_a      (kappa, Kitagawa, somecol)"),
  weight_diag(kw_kit_d1$tau_ml_a1,  "tau_ml_a1/t   (kappa, Kitagawa, somecol)"),
  weight_diag(kw_kit_d1$tau_ml_a0,  "tau_ml_a0     (kappa, Kitagawa, somecol)")
)
print(diag_kit_d1, row.names = FALSE)
##                                 Estimator     Sum_w ESS Pct_neg Max_abs_w
##    PLR-IV        (DML, Kitagawa, somecol) 0.0000000   2    31.8  0.032775
##    Wald-AIPW     (DML, Kitagawa, somecol) 0.0000000   3    31.8  0.065150
##  tau_cb_u      (kappa, Kitagawa, somecol) 0.0000000   3    31.8  0.045670
##  tau_ml_u      (kappa, Kitagawa, somecol) 0.0000000   3    31.8  0.044385
##  tau_ml_a10    (kappa, Kitagawa, somecol) 0.0000000   3    31.8  0.052601
##  tau_ml_a      (kappa, Kitagawa, somecol) 0.3052662   3    31.8  0.041541
##  tau_ml_a1/t   (kappa, Kitagawa, somecol) 0.2787776   4    31.8  0.037937
##  tau_ml_a0     (kappa, Kitagawa, somecol) 0.3865350   2    31.8  0.052601
# --- E4: educ16, Kitagawa spec ---
cat("\n--- Kitagawa specification, educ16 (D2) ---\n")
## 
## --- Kitagawa specification, educ16 (D2) ---
kw_kit_d2 <- kappa_weights_bundle(Z, D2, X_kit_kappa)

diag_kit_d2 <- rbind(
  weight_diag(w_plriv_kit_d2,       "PLR-IV        (DML, Kitagawa, educ16)"),
  weight_diag(w_waldaipw_kit_d2,    "Wald-AIPW     (DML, Kitagawa, educ16)"),
  weight_diag(kw_kit_d2$tau_cb_u,   "tau_cb_u      (kappa, Kitagawa, educ16)"),
  weight_diag(kw_kit_d2$tau_ml_u,   "tau_ml_u      (kappa, Kitagawa, educ16)"),
  weight_diag(kw_kit_d2$tau_ml_a10, "tau_ml_a10    (kappa, Kitagawa, educ16)"),
  weight_diag(kw_kit_d2$tau_ml_a,   "tau_ml_a      (kappa, Kitagawa, educ16)"),
  weight_diag(kw_kit_d2$tau_ml_a1,  "tau_ml_a1/t   (kappa, Kitagawa, educ16)"),
  weight_diag(kw_kit_d2$tau_ml_a0,  "tau_ml_a0     (kappa, Kitagawa, educ16)")
)
print(diag_kit_d2, row.names = FALSE)
##                                Estimator     Sum_w ESS Pct_neg Max_abs_w
##    PLR-IV        (DML, Kitagawa, educ16) 0.0000000   1    31.8  0.056988
##    Wald-AIPW     (DML, Kitagawa, educ16) 0.0000000   1    31.8  0.119277
##  tau_cb_u      (kappa, Kitagawa, educ16) 0.0000000   1    31.8  0.081202
##  tau_ml_u      (kappa, Kitagawa, educ16) 0.0000000   1    31.8  0.078334
##  tau_ml_a10    (kappa, Kitagawa, educ16) 0.0000000   0    31.8  0.133832
##  tau_ml_a      (kappa, Kitagawa, educ16) 0.5863183   1    31.8  0.079788
##  tau_ml_a1/t   (kappa, Kitagawa, educ16) 0.4958309   1    31.8  0.067474
##  tau_ml_a0     (kappa, Kitagawa, educ16) 0.9834616   0    31.8  0.133832
cat("\n")
# ==============================================================================
# BLOCK F — LOVE PLOTS: DML VS KAPPA
# Requires: BLOCKS B and E.
# ==============================================================================
#
# Outcome weights are signed (positive for treated, negative for controls under
# the LATE interpretation). For cobalt::love.plot() we multiply by (2D - 1) to
# map signed outcome weights onto the treated-versus-control balance convention.
#
# We produce Love plots for the Card specification (Block B1: somecol), which
# is the main empirical specification. The Card spec has the richest covariate
# set, so balance differences between estimators are most visible there.
#
# Two panels are shown:
#   F1: All 8 estimators using X_dml_card covariates (somecol treatment)
#   F2: DML-only comparison for Card vs Kitagawa, somecol, to show how the
#       learner adapts differently to the two covariate spaces.
#   F3: All 8 estimators using X_dml_card covariates (educ16 treatment)
#
# NOTE: Love plots for the Kitagawa spec are less informative (only 5 binary
#       covariates with near-identical standardised mean differences across
#       estimators) and are reported in the appendix.

cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## ======================================================================
cat("BLOCK F — LOVE PLOTS: DML VS KAPPA\n")
## BLOCK F — LOVE PLOTS: DML VS KAPPA
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n\n")
## ======================================================================
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Compute kappa weight bundles here so Block F is self-contained.
# These are the same objects assigned in Block E; recomputing here means Block F
# runs correctly even if Block E was skipped or errored.
# kappa_weights_bundle() uses the 3-argument toolkit version re-enforced in
# Block E (or the toolkit directly if Block E ran first — result is identical).
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
kw_card_d1 <- kappa_weights_bundle(Z, D1, X_card_kappa)
kw_card_d2 <- kappa_weights_bundle(Z, D2, X_card_kappa)
kw_kit_d1  <- kappa_weights_bundle(Z, D1, X_kit_kappa)
kw_kit_d2  <- kappa_weights_bundle(Z, D2, X_kit_kappa)

# --- F1: Card spec, somecol — all 8 estimators ---
cat("F1: Love plots — Card specification, somecol (D1)\n\n")
## F1: Love plots — Card specification, somecol (D1)
# Love plot helper for Card spec (somecol, D1)
# Uses X_dml_card for the covariate reference matrix.
make_love_card_d1 <- function(title_str, w_vec) {
  # Multiply by (2D1 - 1) to convert signed outcome weights to the
  # treated-vs-control convention expected by cobalt::love.plot().
  # This is identical to the Vietnam DML Block F approach.
  cobalt::love.plot(
    D1 ~ X_dml_card,
    weights    = w_vec * (2 * D1 - 1),
    position   = "bottom",
    title      = title_str,
    thresholds = c(m = 0.1),
    var.order  = "unadjusted",
    binary     = "std",
    abs        = TRUE,
    line       = TRUE,
    colors     = viridis::viridis(2),
    shapes     = c("circle", "triangle")
  )
}

lp_f1_1 <- make_love_card_d1("PLR-IV (Card, somecol)",        w_plriv_card_d1)
## Note: `s.d.denom` not specified; assuming "pooled".
lp_f1_2 <- make_love_card_d1("Wald-AIPW (Card, somecol)",     w_waldaipw_card_d1)
## Note: `s.d.denom` not specified; assuming "pooled".
lp_f1_3 <- make_love_card_d1("tau_cb_u (Card, somecol)",      kw_card_d1$tau_cb_u)
## Note: `s.d.denom` not specified; assuming "pooled".
lp_f1_4 <- make_love_card_d1("tau_ml_u (Card, somecol)",      kw_card_d1$tau_ml_u)
## Note: `s.d.denom` not specified; assuming "pooled".
lp_f1_5 <- make_love_card_d1("tau_ml_a10 (Card, somecol)",    kw_card_d1$tau_ml_a10)
## Note: `s.d.denom` not specified; assuming "pooled".
lp_f1_6 <- make_love_card_d1("tau_ml_a (Card, somecol)",      kw_card_d1$tau_ml_a)
## Note: `s.d.denom` not specified; assuming "pooled".
lp_f1_7 <- make_love_card_d1("tau_ml_a1/t (Card, somecol)",   kw_card_d1$tau_ml_a1)
## Note: `s.d.denom` not specified; assuming "pooled".
lp_f1_8 <- make_love_card_d1("tau_ml_a0 (Card, somecol)",     kw_card_d1$tau_ml_a0)
## Note: `s.d.denom` not specified; assuming "pooled".
grid.arrange(lp_f1_1, lp_f1_2, lp_f1_3, lp_f1_4,
             lp_f1_5, lp_f1_6, lp_f1_7, lp_f1_8,
             nrow = 2,
             top = "Love plots: DML vs Kappa | Card specification | somecol")

# --- F2: Card vs Kitagawa — DML only, somecol ---
# This panel shows how the random-forest learner adapts to the two different
# covariate spaces. With the Card spec (14 binary/continuous variables) the
# forest can learn region × experience interactions; with Kitagawa (5 binary
# variables) it cannot. Differences in the Love plots tell us whether the
# richer spec achieves better complier balance.

cat("\nF2: Love plots — DML only, Card vs Kitagawa, somecol (D1)\n\n")
## 
## F2: Love plots — DML only, Card vs Kitagawa, somecol (D1)
make_love_kit_d1 <- function(title_str, w_vec) {
  cobalt::love.plot(
    D1 ~ X_dml_kit,
    weights    = w_vec * (2 * D1 - 1),
    position   = "bottom",
    title      = title_str,
    thresholds = c(m = 0.1),
    var.order  = "unadjusted",
    binary     = "std",
    abs        = TRUE,
    line       = TRUE,
    colors     = viridis::viridis(2),
    shapes     = c("circle", "triangle")
  )
}

lp_f2_1 <- make_love_card_d1("PLR-IV (Card spec, somecol)",      w_plriv_card_d1)
## Note: `s.d.denom` not specified; assuming "pooled".
lp_f2_2 <- make_love_card_d1("Wald-AIPW (Card spec, somecol)",   w_waldaipw_card_d1)
## Note: `s.d.denom` not specified; assuming "pooled".
lp_f2_3 <- make_love_kit_d1( "PLR-IV (Kitagawa spec, somecol)",  w_plriv_kit_d1)
## Note: `s.d.denom` not specified; assuming "pooled".
lp_f2_4 <- make_love_kit_d1( "Wald-AIPW (Kitagawa spec, somecol)", w_waldaipw_kit_d1)
## Note: `s.d.denom` not specified; assuming "pooled".
grid.arrange(lp_f2_1, lp_f2_2, lp_f2_3, lp_f2_4,
             nrow = 1,
             top = "Love plots: DML — Card vs Kitagawa specification | somecol")

# --- F3: Card spec, educ16 — all 8 estimators ---
cat("\nF3: Love plots — Card specification, educ16 (D2)\n\n")
## 
## F3: Love plots — Card specification, educ16 (D2)
make_love_card_d2 <- function(title_str, w_vec) {
  cobalt::love.plot(
    D2 ~ X_dml_card,
    weights    = w_vec * (2 * D2 - 1),
    position   = "bottom",
    title      = title_str,
    thresholds = c(m = 0.1),
    var.order  = "unadjusted",
    binary     = "std",
    abs        = TRUE,
    line       = TRUE,
    colors     = viridis::viridis(2),
    shapes     = c("circle", "triangle")
  )
}

lp_f3_1 <- make_love_card_d2("PLR-IV (Card, educ16)",        w_plriv_card_d2)
## Note: `s.d.denom` not specified; assuming "pooled".
lp_f3_2 <- make_love_card_d2("Wald-AIPW (Card, educ16)",     w_waldaipw_card_d2)
## Note: `s.d.denom` not specified; assuming "pooled".
lp_f3_3 <- make_love_card_d2("tau_cb_u (Card, educ16)",      kw_card_d2$tau_cb_u)
## Note: `s.d.denom` not specified; assuming "pooled".
lp_f3_4 <- make_love_card_d2("tau_ml_u (Card, educ16)",      kw_card_d2$tau_ml_u)
## Note: `s.d.denom` not specified; assuming "pooled".
lp_f3_5 <- make_love_card_d2("tau_ml_a10 (Card, educ16)",    kw_card_d2$tau_ml_a10)
## Note: `s.d.denom` not specified; assuming "pooled".
lp_f3_6 <- make_love_card_d2("tau_ml_a (Card, educ16)",      kw_card_d2$tau_ml_a)
## Note: `s.d.denom` not specified; assuming "pooled".
lp_f3_7 <- make_love_card_d2("tau_ml_a1/t (Card, educ16)",   kw_card_d2$tau_ml_a1)
## Note: `s.d.denom` not specified; assuming "pooled".
lp_f3_8 <- make_love_card_d2("tau_ml_a0 (Card, educ16)",     kw_card_d2$tau_ml_a0)
## Note: `s.d.denom` not specified; assuming "pooled".
grid.arrange(lp_f3_1, lp_f3_2, lp_f3_3, lp_f3_4,
             lp_f3_5, lp_f3_6, lp_f3_7, lp_f3_8,
             nrow = 2,
             top = "Love plots: DML vs Kappa | Card specification | educ16")

# ==============================================================================
# BLOCK G — SUMMARY TABLE: DML VS KAPPA POINT ESTIMATES
# Requires: BLOCKS B and C.
# ==============================================================================
#
# Presents point estimates for all estimators side-by-side for the dollars
# outcome. Kappa standard errors come from the M-estimation sandwich formula
# (kappa_analytic_se_all); DML standard errors come from dml_with_smoother()
# summary output. This table is the core empirical result for Chapter 5.
#
# Structure:
#   Rows    : estimators (DML: PLR-IV, Wald-AIPW; Kappa: tau_cb_u, tau_ml_u,
#              tau_ml_a10, tau_ml_a, tau_ml_t, tau_ml_a0)
#   Columns : (somecol | Card), (somecol | Kitagawa), (educ16 | Card), (educ16 | Kit)
#
# All estimates are for Y = lwage = log(wage/100) [dollars].
# Kappa estimates for somecol (Card) match SUW Table 3 cols 2 and 6 exactly
# (i.e. the "dollars" columns). They are pulled from kappa_analytic_se_all()
# which was already run in card_1995_replication.R, or recomputed here freshly.

cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## ======================================================================
cat("BLOCK G — SUMMARY TABLE: DML VS KAPPA POINT ESTIMATES\n")
## BLOCK G — SUMMARY TABLE: DML VS KAPPA POINT ESTIMATES
cat("(log wages in dollars; kappa M-estimation SEs; DML OLS-based SEs)\n")
## (log wages in dollars; kappa M-estimation SEs; DML OLS-based SEs)
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n\n")
## ======================================================================
# Helper: extract PLR-IV and Wald-AIPW from a dml summary matrix
dml_row <- function(res, estimator) {
  coef_val <- get_estimate(res, estimator)
  se_val   <- if ("SE" %in% colnames(res)) as.numeric(res[estimator, "SE"]) else NA_real_
  c(coef = coef_val, se = se_val)
}

# Recompute kappa SEs for dollars outcome (fresh call for self-containedness)
cat("Computing kappa point estimates + SEs (dollars, all 4 cells)...\n")
## Computing kappa point estimates + SEs (dollars, all 4 cells)...
kappa_card_d1 <- kappa_analytic_se_all(Y_dol, Z, D1, X_card_kappa)
kappa_card_d2 <- kappa_analytic_se_all(Y_dol, Z, D2, X_card_kappa)
kappa_kit_d1  <- kappa_analytic_se_all(Y_dol, Z, D1, X_kit_kappa)
kappa_kit_d2  <- kappa_analytic_se_all(Y_dol, Z, D2, X_kit_kappa)
cat("Done.\n\n")
## Done.
# Print function for one cell column
print_cell <- function(coef, se) {
  if (is.na(se) || !is.finite(se) || se <= 0)
    return(sprintf("%7.3f (  NA  )", coef))
  pval  <- 2 * pnorm(-abs(coef / se))
  stars <- ifelse(pval < 0.01, "***", ifelse(pval < 0.05, "**",
           ifelse(pval < 0.10, "*",   "")))
  sprintf("%7.3f%3s (%5.3f)", coef, stars, se)
}

# Column headers
cat(sprintf("%-22s  %-19s  %-19s  %-19s  %-19s\n",
            "Estimator",
            "somecol | Card",
            "somecol | Kitagawa",
            "educ16  | Card",
            "educ16  | Kitagawa"))
## Estimator               somecol | Card       somecol | Kitagawa   educ16  | Card       educ16  | Kitagawa
cat(strrep("-", 100), "\n")
## ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# DML rows
plriv_card_d1   <- dml_row(res_card_d1, "PLR-IV")
plriv_card_d2   <- dml_row(res_card_d2, "PLR-IV")
plriv_kit_d1    <- dml_row(res_kit_d1,  "PLR-IV")
plriv_kit_d2    <- dml_row(res_kit_d2,  "PLR-IV")
waipw_card_d1   <- dml_row(res_card_d1, "Wald-AIPW")
waipw_card_d2   <- dml_row(res_card_d2, "Wald-AIPW")
waipw_kit_d1    <- dml_row(res_kit_d1,  "Wald-AIPW")
waipw_kit_d2    <- dml_row(res_kit_d2,  "Wald-AIPW")

cat(sprintf("%-22s  %s  %s  %s  %s\n", "PLR-IV (DML)",
    print_cell(plriv_card_d1["coef"], plriv_card_d1["se"]),
    print_cell(plriv_kit_d1["coef"],  plriv_kit_d1["se"]),
    print_cell(plriv_card_d2["coef"], plriv_card_d2["se"]),
    print_cell(plriv_kit_d2["coef"],  plriv_kit_d2["se"])))
## PLR-IV (DML)              0.681 ** (0.289)    0.593  * (0.340)    1.140 ** (0.535)    1.030    (0.684)
cat(sprintf("%-22s  %s  %s  %s  %s\n", "Wald-AIPW (DML)",
    print_cell(waipw_card_d1["coef"], waipw_card_d1["se"]),
    print_cell(waipw_kit_d1["coef"],  waipw_kit_d1["se"]),
    print_cell(waipw_card_d2["coef"], waipw_card_d2["se"]),
    print_cell(waipw_kit_d2["coef"],  waipw_kit_d2["se"])))
## Wald-AIPW (DML)           0.279    (0.214)    0.292    (0.249)    0.530    (0.398)    0.535    (0.462)
cat(strrep("-", 100), "\n")
## ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Kappa rows
kappa_estimators <- c("tau_cb_u", "tau_ml_u", "tau_ml_a10",
                       "tau_ml_a", "tau_ml_t", "tau_ml_a0")
kappa_labels     <- c("tau_cb_u (kappa)",   "tau_ml_u (kappa)",
                       "tau_ml_a10 (kappa)", "tau_ml_a (kappa)",
                       "tau_ml_t (kappa)",   "tau_ml_a0 (kappa)")

for (i in seq_along(kappa_estimators)) {
  est <- kappa_estimators[i]
  cat(sprintf("%-22s  %s  %s  %s  %s\n",
      kappa_labels[i],
      print_cell(kappa_card_d1$estimates[est], kappa_card_d1$se[est]),
      print_cell(kappa_kit_d1$estimates[est],  kappa_kit_d1$se[est]),
      print_cell(kappa_card_d2$estimates[est], kappa_card_d2$se[est]),
      print_cell(kappa_kit_d2$estimates[est],  kappa_kit_d2$se[est])))
}
## tau_cb_u (kappa)          0.376  * (0.223)    0.331    (0.236)    0.853    (0.549)    0.588    (0.433)
## tau_ml_u (kappa)          0.331    (0.202)    0.356    (0.244)    0.619    (0.387)    0.628    (0.448)
## tau_ml_a10 (kappa)        0.346  * (0.200)    0.293    (0.252)    0.586  * (0.356)    0.836    (0.821)
## tau_ml_a (kappa)          0.170    (0.370)    0.842 ** (0.362)    0.315    (0.696)    1.617  * (0.891)
## tau_ml_t (kappa)          0.171    (0.367)    0.769 ** (0.308)    0.319    (0.687)    1.367 ** (0.648)
## tau_ml_a0 (kappa)         0.154    (0.354)    1.066  * (0.574)    0.266    (0.639)    2.712    (2.577)
cat(strrep("-", 100), "\n")
## ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("Notes:\n")
## Notes:
cat("  DML: PLR-IV and Wald-AIPW from dml_with_smoother() (OutcomeWeights package).\n")
##   DML: PLR-IV and Wald-AIPW from dml_with_smoother() (OutcomeWeights package).
cat("       5-fold cross-fitting, tune.parameters='all', grf random forest.\n")
##        5-fold cross-fitting, tune.parameters='all', grf random forest.
cat("  Kappa: M-estimation sandwich SEs from SUW (2025) online appendix.\n")
##   Kappa: M-estimation sandwich SEs from SUW (2025) online appendix.
cat("  All estimates for Y = log(wage/100) [dollars before log].\n")
##   All estimates for Y = log(wage/100) [dollars before log].
cat("  DML run independently for each (treatment, spec) combination.\n")
##   DML run independently for each (treatment, spec) combination.
cat("  Significance: *** p<0.01, ** p<0.05, * p<0.10 (two-sided z-test).\n")
##   Significance: *** p<0.01, ** p<0.05, * p<0.10 (two-sided z-test).
cat("\n")
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## ======================================================================
cat("DML EXTENSION COMPLETE.\n")
## DML EXTENSION COMPLETE.
cat(strrep("=", 70), "\n")
## ======================================================================
